data.content:
The build confirms the file is generated (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`) but the compiler doesn't see it. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the file but **never adds it to the `Compile` item group**, so MSBuild doesn't pass it to the C# compiler.

Fix: add a `<Compile>` item for the generated file inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it never adds the generated file to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild collects `Compile` items before `CoreCompile`, so even though the file is generated, it's not included in the compilation.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item inside the target so the generated file gets included in the compilation.

arguments.new_str:
<!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />"}

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `<Compile>` item group. MSBuild only compiles files that are in that item group, so the generated file was silently ignored.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
